title: carex glacialis
intro:
Carex glacialis, also known as Glacier Sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae) that grows in wet, cold environments, typically found in mountainous regions.

It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of various gastrointestinal issues. The plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits. In herbal preparations, Carex glacialis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.
